profile.name: Hafis Umar-Lawal
profile.title: Power Electronics & Hardware Engineer
profile.about:
I design and debug the power electronics and hardware that connect solar panels to homes, batteries to grids, and empower satellite missions in low Earth orbit.

Over the past decade, I have developed complex hardware across dcbel, GBatteries, and academia, producing over 25 PCBs. I have been the lead designer for two fully industrialized products and taken complete ownership of critical, standalone subsystems within product platforms. My work spans both commercial markets and internal R&D—from residential microgrid platforms and module-level solar optimizers to specialized, high-bandwidth GaN battery cyclers. I am a certified TÜV SÜD Functional Safety Engineer, and thrive on guiding my hardware from concept through schematic capture, board layout, prototype bring-up, failure forensics, regulatory certification (UL, FCC, IEC), and manufacturing handover.

Before my work in industry, I conducted research on single-stage solar micro-inverters at the University of Manitoba's Renewable Energy Interface and Grid Automation (RIGA) Lab. Concurrently, through the university's Space Technologies and Advanced Research (STAR) Lab, I served as the Lead Avionics Developer for the IRIS CubeSat—a nanosatellite mission developed as part of the Canadian Space Agency’s Canadian CubeSat Project.
profile.cv_summary: Hardware engineer delivering high-density power converters from concept through safety certification and manufacturing handover. Core focus areas: bidirectional converter design (up to 14kW), first-principles failure forensics, and PLM/CAD governance. Certified TÜV SÜD Functional Safety Engineer.

pillars.title: Power Conversion Stage Design
pillars.description: Designing bidirectional high-density converters (up to 800V, 14kW class, GaN power stages to 500kHz), managing critical clearance and creepage, and engineering core sub-circuits including precharge loops, relay economization, DESAT protection, and tight layout loops.

pillars.title: Failure Analysis & Diagnostics (Forensics)
pillars.description: Applying first-principles physics to debug transient anomalies across multi-board assemblies, systematically narrowing the solution space to resolve complex root causes ranging from false desaturation detection, charge pump starvation, and relay dropout, to EMI/EMC compliance issues.

pillars.title: Operational Coordination & CAD Management
pillars.description: Managing CAD component library databases (Altium 365 DBLib), establishing component and schematic templates, and gatekeeping electronics release pipelines via ECO workflows in PLM software (Arena).

education.degree: Master of Science (M.Sc.) in Electrical and Computer Engineering
courses: Modern Control for Power Electronic Systems

education.degree: Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) in Electrical Engineering (With Distinction)
education.honors: Graduated with a DGPA of 3.80 or higher

certifications.name: Functional Safety Engineer
certifications.details: Certified Functional Safety Engineer; applied safety lifecycle governance to residential microgrid software and hardware designs to satisfy UL 60730 Class B requirements.

certifications.name: EMC Filter Design
certifications.details: Focused on the analytical design of input/output DC/DC and AC/DC EMI filters to meet compliance specifications for EU/FCC conducted emission tests.

certifications.name: Design for EMI/EMC
certifications.details: Product layout and circuit design methodology to mitigate electromagnetic interference, utilizing circuit simulations and hardware debugging tools.

patents.title: Photovoltaic power optimizer with overvoltage protection control and rapid shutdown device
patents.details: Co-invented with R. Hesaraki, E. Montllo Casabayo, and C. Wauthy. Assigned to dcbel.

publications.title: Design and implementation of a grid-connected solar micro-inverter using a single-stage galvanically isolated topology with integrated magnetics
An Isolated Single Stage Single Phase Micro-Inverter topology with integrated magnetics
publications.publisher: IEEE Energy Conversion Congress and Exposition (ECCE)
